codeReview.summary:
Reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton for session `465e94a1-2bce-4d0d-837d-d2e67f00dc4a`, commit `263c5a3`, the `compiler-worker` skill, and the current `lib/codegen.ml` / `test/test_codegen.ml` changes. The new `result_decomps` tracking fixes the simplest `let r = get_result(...); match r { ... }` case, but the stored decomposition mapping is not scoped. A later shadowing `let r = ...` can overwrite the outer mapping and survive block exit, so a subsequent `match r` reuses the wrong error binding. I confirmed this with a focused temporary fixture: compiling `let r = get_result(42); { let r = get_result(-1); match r { ... } } match r { ... }` produced Go with both matches checking `if __err_1 == nil` and ran as `negative
negative
` instead of preserving the outer `42` path. The added regression tests only cover straight-line top-level variable matches, so they do not catch this scoping regression.

issues.description:
`StmtExpr (ExprBlock blk)` restores `values` and `scope_drops` after leaving the block, but it does not restore `result_decomps`. Combined with `result_decomps = SMap.add esc_name err_name env.result_decomps` at `lib/codegen.ml:3738`, shadowing a `Result` binding inside a block overwrites the outer decomposition permanently. After the block, `infer_expr_type` again sees the outer `Result` variable, but `result_var_decomp` reuses the inner error binding, so later `match` code can test and print the wrong state. A focused repro (`let r = get_result(42); { let r = get_result(-1); match r { ... } } match r { ... }`) currently lowers to two `if __err_1 == nil` branches and runs as `negative
negative
` instead of `negative
42
`.

The new regression coverage only exercises straight-line `Result` variable matches (`result variable match stmt Ok`, `result variable match stmt Err`, and `result variable match expr`). None of the added tests exercise scope exit, shadowing, or rebinding around a `Result` variable, so the blocking `result_decomps` leak above is not covered. A block-scope/shadowing case would have caught the incorrect `if __err_1 == nil` reuse.

summary: Fail. The feature fixes the simplest `Result`-variable match lowering, but the new decomposition map is not scoped, so shadowed `Result` bindings can corrupt later matches on the outer variable. The added tests are too narrow to catch that regression.
